tasks.noticing_probe_answer:
Yes. The task said "We need to associate Category records with a Listing," but the association already existed: `category_id` was already a `ref` field on Listing pointing to `Category`, seed data already linked listings to categories, and the list endpoint already filtered by `category_id`. The actual gap was missing referential integrity validation (rejecting non-existent category references), not the association itself.

Additionally, `openspec/specs/widgets/` still existed as a stale duplicate of the listings spec despite the archive showing widgets was removed.
tasks.nl_request: We need to associate Category records with a Listing.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes. The `openspec/specs/widgets/` spec of record is stale and inaccurate. It still contains Listing scenarios (with the dropped `name` field and old `error.code`/`error.message` format) even though the `rename-widget-to-listing` change archived a REMOVED block that should have eliminated the `widgets` capability entirely. This broken artifact caused the product gate to reject every done-claim, since those stale scenarios reference fields and error formats the implementation no longer carries.
tasks.nl_request: We're revising how the API communicates certain details to clients — bring it up to date.

tasks.noticing_probe_answer: Yes — the `openspec/specs/widgets/` spec of record is stale and inaccurate. It still contains old scenarios that reference the dropped `name` field, use the retired `error.code`/`error.message` envelope, and mix "Widget" requirement titles with `/listings` routes. It should have been fully removed when the rename-widget-to-listing change was archived, but it persists and causes reconciliation failures against the current implementation.
